content: Re-read live orchestrator-role/SKILL.md and confirm exact locations of Colony Law references. Found two: (a) prose reference to Law 2 using a 7-level '../../../../../../../.agents/rules/02-pmcro-cycle-discipline.md' chain; (b) Invocation Contract's repo_path line referencing Law 1 as bare '.agents/rules/01-declarative-params.md' (no ../.. prefix). Success condition: both located and quoted verbatim before editing.

Replace the Law 2 relative chain with '<repo_path>/.agents/rules/02-pmcro-cycle-discipline.md'. Leave the Law 1 repo_path description wording alone in this step -- it has no '../../' to replace, and per plan step 4 it must instead match the dispatcher's own wording verbatim, not be independently reformatted. Success condition: file re-read, Law 2 reference confirmed changed, no other prose altered.

Rename the 'Invocation Contract' heading to 'Usage (System Contract)'. Read commands/run-cycle.md (the actual dispatcher that invokes this role-skill) and diff its parameter block against this section's own domain/true_intent/repo_path block. Success condition: field names and repo_path resolution wording match the dispatcher's block exactly (dispatcher already uses identical field names -- confirm no drift rather than assume).

In the 'Reference Files' list at the bottom of the file, replace each '../../references/*.md' entry with a stable, full repo-relative path from repo root (catalog/Platform/PMCR-O/skills/orchestrator/references/*.md). Do not touch the inline '../../references/...' mentions embedded in step 1/2 prose earlier in the file -- true_intent scoped this change to the Reference Files section specifically; flag the inline mentions as an open note for a future cycle rather than silently expanding scope.

Re-read the full file after all edits to verify: no remaining '../../' Colony-Law reference, heading renamed, parameters match dispatcher, Reference Files section uses full repo-relative paths, nothing else in the file altered. Success condition: diff limited to exactly these four concerns.
